volumeTitle: The Merchant. The Braggart Soldier. The Ghost. The Persian
label: The Ghost (Section 1.4.333d - Section 2.1.380)

sections.label: Section 1.4.333d
sections.translation: I know .

sections.label: Section 1.4.334
sections.translation: It has just come into my head :

sections.label: Section 1.4.335
sections.translation: why , of course I ’ m going home for a booze .

sections.label: Section 1.4.335ab
sections.translation: Why yes , really now I do remember that .

sections.label: Section 1.4.336
sections.original: Núm non vis me óbviam hís ire , anime mi ?
sections.translation: Won ’ t you let me go to find them , my life ? Of all persons I wish well to him especially . I ’ ll return just now .

sections.label: Section 1.4.338b
sections.translation: That just now is a long time to me .

sections.label: Section 1.4.339
sections.translation: Is there any person here ?

sections.label: Section 1.4.339b
sections.translation: ’ Tis he .

sections.label: Section 1.4.339c
sections.translation: Bravo ! Philolaches ,

sections.label: Section 1.4.340
sections.translation: good day to you , most friendly to me of all men .

sections.label: Section 1.4.341
sections.translation: May the Gods bless you . ( Pointing to a couch .) Take your place , Callidamates . ( He takes his place .) Whence are you betaking yourself ?

sections.label: Section 1.4.342b
sections.translation: Whence a drunken man does .

sections.label: Section 1.4.343
sections.translation: Well said . But , my Delphium , do take your place , there ’ s a dear .

sections.label: Section 1.4.344
sections.translation: Give her something to drink .

sections.label: Section 1.4.344b
sections.translation: I shall go to sleep directly .

sections.label: Section 1.4.345
sections.original: Núm mirum aut novom quíppiam facit ?
sections.translation: He doesn ’ t do anything wonderful or strange .

sections.label: Section 1.4.346
sections.translation: What shall I do with him then , my dear ?

sections.label: Section 1.4.346b
sections.translation: Let him alone just as he is .

sections.label: Section 1.4.347
sections.original: age tu ínterim da ab Délphio cito cántharum circum .
sections.translation: Come , you boy . Meanwhile , speedily pass the goblet round , beginning with Delphium .

sections.label: Section 2.1.348
sections.original: Iúppiter supremus summis opibus atque industriis
sections.translation: Supreme Jove , with all his might and resources , is seeking for me and Philolaches , my master ’ s son , to be undone .

sections.label: Section 2.1.350
sections.original: occidit Spes nostra , nusquam stabulum est Confidentiae ,
sections.translation: Our hopes are destroyed ; nowhere is there any hold for courage ; not even Salvation [*] now could save us if she wished . Such an immense mountain of woe have I just now seen at the harbour : my master has arrived from abroad ; Tranio is undone ! ( To the . AUDIENCE .) Is there any person who ’ d like to make gain of a little money ,

sections.label: Section 2.1.355
sections.original: quí hodie sese excruciari meam vicem possit pati ?
sections.translation: who could this day endure to take my place in being tortured ? Where are those fellows hardened to a flogging , the wearers-out of iron chains , or those , who , for the consideration of three didrachms , would get beneath besieging towers [*] , where some are in the way of having their bodies pierced with fifteen spears ? I ’ ll give a talent to that man who shall be the first to run to the cross for me ;

sections.label: Section 2.1.360
sections.original: sed ea lege , ut offigantur bis pedes , bis bracchia .
sections.translation: but on condition that twice his feet , twice his arms [*] are fastened there . When that shall have been done , then ask the money down of me . But am I not a wretched fellow , not at full speed to be running home ?

sections.label: Section 2.1.363
sections.original: Adest adest opsonium . eccum Tranio a portu redit .
sections.translation: Here come the provisions ; see , here ’ s Tranio ; he ’ s come back from the harbour .

sections.label: Section 2.1.364
sections.translation: Philolaches !

sections.label: Section 2.1.364b
sections.translation: What ’ s the matter ?

sections.label: Section 2.1.364c
sections.translation: Both I and you —

sections.label: Section 2.1.364d
sections.translation: What about Both I and you ?

sections.label: Section 2.1.364e
sections.translation: Are undone !

sections.label: Section 2.1.365
sections.translation: Why so ?

sections.label: Section 2.1.365b
sections.translation: Your father ’ s here .

sections.label: Section 2.1.365c
sections.translation: What is it I hear of you ?

sections.label: Section 2.1.365d
sections.translation: We are finished up . Your father ’ s come , I say .

sections.label: Section 2.1.366b
sections.translation: Where is he , I do entreat you ?

sections.label: Section 2.1.366c
sections.translation: He ’ s coming .

sections.label: Section 2.1.367
sections.translation: Coming ? Who says so ? Who has seen him ?

sections.label: Section 2.1.367b
sections.translation: I saw him myself , I tell you .

sections.label: Section 2.1.367c
sections.translation: Woe unto me ! what am I about ?

sections.label: Section 2.1.368b
sections.original: Nam quid tu , malum , me rogitas quid agas ? accubas .
sections.translation: Why the plague now do you ask me what you are about ? Taking your place at table , of course .

sections.label: Section 2.1.369
sections.translation: Did you see him ?

sections.label: Section 2.1.369b
sections.translation: I my own self , I tell you .

sections.label: Section 2.1.369c
sections.translation: For certain ?

sections.label: Section 2.1.369d
sections.translation: For certain , I tell you .

sections.label: Section 2.1.369e
sections.translation: I ’ m undone ,

sections.label: Section 2.1.370
sections.translation: if you are telling the truth .

sections.label: Section 2.1.370b
sections.original: Quid mihi sit boni , si mentiar ?
sections.translation: What good could it be to me if I told a lie ?

sections.label: Section 2.1.371
sections.translation: What shall I do now ?

sections.label: Section 2.1.371b
sections.translation: Order all these things to be removed from here . ( Pointing .) Who ’ s that asleep there ?

sections.label: Section 2.1.372b
sections.translation: Callidamates .

sections.label: Section 2.1.372c
sections.translation: Arouse him , Delphium .

sections.label: Section 2.1.373
sections.translation: Callidamates ! Callidamates ! awake !

sections.label: Section 2.1.373b
sections.translation: I am awake ; give me something to drink .

sections.label: Section 2.1.374
sections.translation: Awake ; the father of Philolaches has arrived from abroad .

sections.label: Section 2.1.374b
sections.translation: I hope his father ’ s well .

sections.label: Section 2.1.375
sections.original: Valet ille quidem , atque ego disperii .
sections.translation: He is well indeed ; but I am utterly undone .

sections.label: Section 2.1.375b
sections.translation: You , utterly undone ? How can that be ?

sections.label: Section 2.1.376
sections.translation: By heavens ! do get up , I beg of you ; my father has arrived .

sections.label: Section 2.1.376b
sections.translation: Your father has come ? Bid him go back again . What business had he to come back here so soon ?

sections.label: Section 2.1.378
sections.original: Quid ego agam ? pater iam híc me offendet miserum adveniens ebrium ,
sections.translation: What am I to do ? My father will , just now , be coming and unfortunately finding me amid drunken carousals , and the house full of revellers and women . It ’ s a shocking bad job ,

sections.label: Section 2.1.380
sections.original: igitur demum fodere puteum , úbi sitis fauces tenet ;
sections.translation: to be digging a well at the last moment , just when thirst has gained possession of your throat ; just as I , on the arrival of my father , wretch that I am , am now enquiring what I am to do .
